About Me
QA Tester
gonzalesrichmond321@gmail.com +63 910 709 1630 Cebu City, Cebu, Philippines

Committed to Quality

I earned my Bachelor of Science in Information Technology from the State University of Northern Negros and have spent the last 3+ years testing web and mobile applications throughout the Software Testing Life Cycle (STLC). As both a feature tester and a member of a dedicated bug verification team, I've executed complete manual testing cycles, created structured test cases, verified hundreds of defects, and supported stable feature releases in Agile environments.

I've always enjoyed designing and building applications, especially with the help of AI assistants. But what I enjoy even more is making sure those applications work reliably and provide a great experience for users. Seeing an application run smoothly with minimal bugs is what inspired me to pursue a career in Quality Assurance.

Knowing that I've helped improve the quality of an application is what makes this work rewarding. That's why I focus on understanding requirements, finding issues early, and working closely with developers to deliver software users can trust.

What I can do for your team

  • Deliver reliable feature testing
  • Prevent production defects
  • Track and verify bugs
  • Support faster deployments

How I Work

My QA Process

01

Requirement Analysis

Review user stories & feature requirements to understand what needs to be tested and identify potential issues early.

02

Test Planning

Plan what needs to be tested and how it will be tested

03

Test Case Creation

Create clear and detailed test cases based on feature requirements to ensure every functionality is tested thoroughly.

04

Test Execution

Execute test cases to verify that features work as expected and identify defects before release.

05

Bug Reporting

Document clear & detailed bug reports with accurate reproduction steps to help developers resolve issues efficiently.

06

Regression Testing

Retest existing features after changes to ensure everything works correctly & no new issues are introduced.

07

Release Validation

Verify that features and bug fixes are fully tested before and after each release.

Projects

Featured Work

Featured Project  ·  Android App (Kotlin)
Mobile App  ·  Kotlin  ·  Supabase / PostgreSQL

SpendHound 🐾

SpendHound is a financial tracking and expense management app designed to help users monitor, organize, and settle shared or personal expenses efficiently. It likely focuses on: Tracking daily spending, Managing shared transactions (e.g., with friends, groups, or teams), Handling settlements between users (who owes what), Providing a clean, intuitive interface for financial clarity.

As the QA Tester, I owned the quality assurance process across all feature modules in SpendHound — writing test cases, executing functional and regression testing, and validating app stability prior to deployment.

Key QA Responsibilities

  • Executed full manual testing cycles covering functional, UI/UX, compatibility, regression, smoke & sanity testing across Agile sprint cycles
  • Verified key defect tickets as part of the bug team, identifying critical issues before production
  • Created structured test cases in TestRail/Spreadsheets covering all modules: Auth, Biometrics, Groups, Expenses, Borrow Tracker, Chat, Charts, Settings
  • Logged defects in JIRA with clear reproduction steps, severity ratings, device specs & screenshots
  • Performed API spot-checks via Postman against Supabase REST endpoints for transactions, groups, and borrow data
  • Used SQL queries against PostgreSQL/Supabase to validate balance calculations, borrow records & data integrity
  • Validated biometric auth flows, skeleton loading states, archived transactions, and real-time Supabase sync
  • Conducted targeted regression testing after the March 2026 database schema alignment update

Modules Tested

User AuthBiometricsGroups & Crew Expense TrackingBorrow TrackerDebt Settlement Chat & MessagingData Visualization Archived TransactionsReal-time SyncProfile & Settings

Sample QA Test Cases

Structured test cases for the SpendHound app covering all major modules. Full suite available on GitHub.

Bug Reports

Sample Bug Reports

JIRA-style defect reports logged during SpendHound QA cycles. Full reports on GitHub.

BUG-001 Critical Fixed

App crashes when saving borrow record with decimal amount

Borrow Tracker Android 13 · Pixel 6 Build v1.2.0

Steps to Reproduce:

  1. Open Borrow Tracker → Tap "Add New Record"
  2. Enter amount: 150.50
  3. Fill remaining fields and tap Save

Expected: Record saved with decimal amount (₱150.50)

Actual: App crashes with unhandled NumberFormatException; no record saved

BUG-002 Critical Fixed

Group balance total incorrect after member is removed

Group Management Android & Web Build v1.3.1

Steps to Reproduce:

  1. Create a group with 3 members; add ₱900 shared expense
  2. Remove Member C from the group
  3. View the group spending summary

Expected: Balances recalculated for remaining 2 members (₱450 each)

Actual: Removed member's ₱300 share still included; totals incorrect

BUG-003 High Fixed

Real-time sync does not reflect new chat messages without app restart

Chat / Real-time Sync Android 12+ Build v1.4.0

Steps to Reproduce:

  1. Open group chat on Device A (app in foreground)
  2. Send a message from Device B
  3. Observe Device A without restarting

Expected: New message appears in real-time via Supabase subscription

Actual: Message only appears after manual app restart; Supabase realtime listener not re-subscribing correctly

BUG-004 High Fixed

Skeleton loading state remains indefinitely on slow/no network

UI / Loading States Android 12+ Build v1.3.0

Steps to Reproduce:

  1. Enable network throttling (simulate 2G or offline)
  2. Navigate to Expense Feed or Borrow Tracker
  3. Wait 30+ seconds

Expected: Timeout error shown after 15s with "Retry" option

Actual: Skeleton shimmer animation loops indefinitely; no timeout or error state triggered

BUG-005 High Fixed

Biometric login reverts to password screen after OS-level fingerprint update

Auth / Biometrics Android 13 · Various devices Build v1.4.2

Steps to Reproduce:

  1. Enable biometric login in app Settings
  2. Go to Android Settings → add a new fingerprint to device
  3. Return to SpendHound and attempt biometric login

Expected: App detects updated biometric enrollment; prompts user to re-register or continues authentication

Actual: App silently reverts to password login with no explanation or re-enrollment prompt

BUG-006 Medium Fixed

Data visualization chart renders blank after 0-balance debt settlement

Data Visualization Android 11+ Build v1.3.5

Steps to Reproduce:

  1. Settle all expenses in a group (all balances = ₱0)
  2. Navigate to Group Spending Chart

Expected: Empty state shown: "All settled! No outstanding balances."

Actual: MPAndroidChart renders blank white canvas; no empty state UI; looks like a crash to the user

BUG-007 Medium Fixed

Archived transactions reappear in main feed after app force-close and reopen

Expense / Archive Android 12 · Samsung Galaxy Build v1.5.0

Steps to Reproduce:

  1. Archive 2–3 transactions from the Expense Feed
  2. Force-close the app from the recent apps tray
  3. Reopen SpendHound and navigate to Expense Feed

Expected: Archived transactions remain in the Archive tab; not in main feed

Actual: Archived transactions reappear in main feed; archive state not persisted to local Room database on force-close

BUG-008 Medium Fixed

Group invite notification not received on iOS when app is in killed state

iOS 16 · iPhone 13 Notifications / Groups Build v1.4.1

Steps to Reproduce:

  1. Fully kill SpendHound on iOS (swipe up from app switcher)
  2. From another device, add the iOS user to a new group
  3. Wait 60 seconds and check the iOS device

Expected: Push notification: "You've been added to [Group Name]" received

Actual: No notification received while app is killed; notification appears only when app is reopened manually

BUG-009 Low Fixed

Profile photo upload fails silently when file size exceeds 5MB

Settings / Profile Android 13 Build v1.5.1

Steps to Reproduce:

  1. Go to Profile Settings → Tap "Change Photo"
  2. Select a photo larger than 5MB from gallery
  3. Confirm selection

Expected: Error message: "Photo too large. Please choose an image under 5MB."

Actual: Upload spinner appears and disappears; old photo remains with no error message shown

BUG-010 Low Open

Expense date picker defaults to current date even when editing a past expense

Expense / UI Android 12+ Build v1.5.2

Steps to Reproduce:

  1. Open an existing expense created 5 days ago
  2. Tap "Edit Expense"
  3. Observe the date picker field

Expected: Date picker pre-populates with the expense's original date

Actual: Date picker always defaults to today's date; original date not pre-filled when editing

SQL Validation

SQL Data Validations

SQL queries used to validate test data in the SpendHound PostgreSQL/Supabase database — verifying balance calculations, data integrity, and schema correctness. Full queries on GitHub.

Validate expense equal-split calculation expenses · expense_splits 
-- Verify equal-split amounts match expected per-member share
SELECT
  e.id          AS expense_id,
  e.title,
  e.total_amount,
  e.split_count,
  ROUND(e.total_amount / e.split_count, 2) AS expected_per_member,
  es.amount     AS actual_per_member,
  es.user_id,
  CASE
    WHEN ROUND(e.total_amount / e.split_count, 2) = es.amount
      THEN 'PASS'
    ELSE 'FAIL – Amount mismatch'
  END AS result
FROM expenses e
JOIN expense_splits es ON e.id = es.expense_id
WHERE e.split_type = 'equal'
ORDER BY e.created_at DESC;
Verify borrow record status values borrow_records 
-- Validate all borrow records have allowed status values
SELECT
  id,
  lender_id,
  borrower_id,
  amount,
  status,
  updated_at,
  CASE
    WHEN status IN ('pending', 'settled')
      THEN 'PASS'
    ELSE 'FAIL – Invalid status: ' || status
  END AS validation_result
FROM borrow_records
ORDER BY created_at DESC;
Validate group balance totals groups · transactions 
-- Verify group.total_balance matches SUM of transactions
SELECT
  g.id              AS group_id,
  g.name            AS group_name,
  g.total_balance   AS stored_total,
  ROUND(SUM(t.amount), 2) AS calculated_total,
  CASE
    WHEN g.total_balance = ROUND(SUM(t.amount), 2)
      THEN 'PASS'
    ELSE 'FAIL – Balance mismatch'
  END AS result
FROM groups g
JOIN transactions t ON t.group_id = g.id
GROUP BY g.id, g.name, g.total_balance
ORDER BY g.created_at DESC;
Detect orphaned expense splits after member removal expense_splits · group_members 
-- Find expense splits for users no longer in the group
SELECT
  es.id         AS split_id,
  es.expense_id,
  es.user_id,
  e.group_id,
  'FAIL – Orphaned split; user not in group' AS issue
FROM expense_splits es
JOIN expenses e ON es.expense_id = e.id
WHERE es.user_id NOT IN (
  SELECT gm.user_id
  FROM group_members gm
  WHERE gm.group_id = e.group_id
    AND gm.is_active = TRUE
);
-- Expected: 0 rows returned
Verify settled debts reflect zero outstanding balance borrow_records · users 
-- Confirm settled borrow records show 0 net outstanding
SELECT
  br.id,
  u1.display_name   AS lender,
  u2.display_name   AS borrower,
  br.amount,
  br.status,
  CASE
    WHEN br.status = 'settled'
      THEN 'PASS – No outstanding balance'
    WHEN br.status = 'pending' AND br.amount > 0
      THEN 'INFO – Pending repayment: ' || br.amount
    ELSE 'FAIL – Unexpected state'
  END AS validation_result
FROM borrow_records br
JOIN users u1 ON br.lender_id  = u1.id
JOIN users u2 ON br.borrower_id = u2.id
ORDER BY br.updated_at DESC;
Check for duplicate active expense records expenses 
-- Detect duplicate expenses in the same group
SELECT
  group_id,
  title,
  amount,
  created_by,
  DATE(created_at) AS expense_date,
  COUNT(*)          AS duplicate_count,
  CASE
    WHEN COUNT(*) > 1 THEN 'FAIL – Duplicate detected'
    ELSE 'PASS'
  END AS result
FROM expenses
WHERE is_archived = FALSE
GROUP BY group_id, title, amount, created_by, DATE(created_at)
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY duplicate_count DESC;
Validate archived flag not affecting active balances expenses · expense_splits 
-- Confirm archived expenses are excluded from balance totals
SELECT
  g.id             AS group_id,
  g.name,
  g.total_balance  AS stored_balance,
  ROUND(SUM(
    CASE WHEN e.is_archived = FALSE
         THEN es.amount ELSE 0 END
  ), 2)            AS active_only_total,
  CASE
    WHEN g.total_balance = ROUND(SUM(
      CASE WHEN e.is_archived = FALSE
           THEN es.amount ELSE 0 END), 2)
    THEN 'PASS'
    ELSE 'FAIL – Archived items affecting balance'
  END AS result
FROM groups g
JOIN expenses e       ON e.group_id  = g.id
JOIN expense_splits es ON es.expense_id = e.id
GROUP BY g.id, g.name, g.total_balance;
Schema alignment check — March 2026 migration information_schema 
-- Verify all required columns exist after schema migration
SELECT
  table_name,
  column_name,
  data_type,
  is_nullable,
  CASE
    WHEN column_name IN (
      'id','created_at','updated_at','user_id',
      'group_id','amount','status','is_archived'
    ) THEN 'PASS – Required column present'
    ELSE 'INFO – Additional column'
  END AS check_result
FROM information_schema.columns
WHERE table_schema = 'public'
  AND table_name IN (
    'users','groups','expenses',
    'expense_splits','borrow_records',
    'transactions','group_members'
  )
ORDER BY table_name, ordinal_position;
